_README: The ONLY hand-edited file: masthead, about/FAQ/contact, donate (Zelle), sponsor options, and the phone line. Nothing here re-hosts a shiur. Use Editor Mode (pencil, top-left) to edit + download.

masthead.subtitle: Daf Yomi · Beis Medrash Ohr Chaim, Monsey

options._preferSelfHosted_note: When media/manifest.json has our own intro-trimmed copy of a shiur, play that instead of TorahAnytime's.
options._mediaBaseUrl_note: GO-LIVE SWITCH. Empty = serve self-hosted media from THIS site (local media/ folder). Set to a server/CDN base (e.g. https://media.example.com) to serve the same files from there — one-line change, no reprocessing. Manifest paths stay relative (media/<id>.mp3).

phone.label: Listen by Phone
phone.note: Call the TorahAnytime listen line, then dial the Rabbi's extension. (Confirm the access number with TorahAnytime before publishing.)

contact.note: Questions, dedications, or to sponsor a shiur — reach out any time.

donate.heading: Support the Shiur
donate.blurb: The Daf Yomi shiur is given and shared completely free of charge. If it has added to your learning, a contribution helps keep it going — and a dedication can be l'ilui nishmas a loved one or as a zechus for a refuah or simcha.

zelle.name: JOSHUA STERN
zelle._qrData_note: Exact Zelle payment payload (decoded from the original QR). The app renders this as crisp SVG; if removed, it is rebuilt from name+email. 'qr' jpeg kept only as a last-resort fallback.

donate.dedicationNote: To dedicate a day, a masechta, or the whole shiur, email jshmuelstern@gmail.com.

sponsor.heading: Sponsor the Shiur
sponsor.blurb: Dedicate a day of learning — today's daf, an upcoming daf for a yahrtzeit or simcha, or an entire masechta. Your dedication is shared with everyone who learns it.
sponsor.note: After sending your dedication below, complete the sponsorship by Zelle on the Donate page. The Rabbi will confirm and announce the dedication.

about.heading: About the Shiur
paragraphs: Rabbi Shea Stern is a Daf Hayomi Maggid Shiur at Scheiner's Beis Medrash Ohr Chaim in Monsey. He learned in Yeshivas Novominsk and is a talmid of R' Dovid Soloveitchik zt"l, and gives a nightly Oraysa shiur at Khal Bais Avrohom.

This library brings his entire Daf Yomi cycle together in one place — every daf, in order, following the worldwide Daf Yomi calendar. Yesterday's daf, today's daf, and the daf to come are always one tap away.

tradition.heading: Daf Yomi — A Gemara Tradition
tradition.body: Daf Yomi — literally 'the daily page' — is the worldwide practice of learning the same page of Talmud every day, finishing all of Shas in a seven-and-a-half-year cycle that ends with the Siyum HaShas. The cycle begins with Berachos and ends with Niddah. With this library it's easy to keep pace: listen on the way to work, in the car, on the train, or wherever you are.

faqs.a: Yes — every shiur is free to listen to.

faqs.q: How do I find today's daf?
faqs.a: Open the Home tab. Today's daf (and yesterday's and tomorrow's) is shown at the top, following the worldwide Daf Yomi calendar.

faqs.q: Where can I find an older daf?
faqs.a: Tap 'Select a Daf' and choose the Seder, then the Masechta, then the daf. Every daf the Rabbi has taught is there.

faqs.q: Can I see the actual daf?
faqs.a: Yes — tap any daf and choose 'Read the daf' to see the full Talmud text (Hebrew, English, or both), served right here in the app.
Yes — see 'Sponsor the Shiur' in the menu. You can dedicate today's daf, a future daf, or a whole masechta.

faqs.q: Does the library update on its own?
faqs.a: Yes. New shiurim appear automatically as they're posted — there is nothing to refresh or install.

seriesIntros.Chullin: The laws of shechita, kashrus of meat, and the mitzvos of everyday Jewish living.
